scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with valid HTML delegates to treeBuilder.parse and returns a non-null Document (baseUri applied)
scenarios.expectedResult:
return value is a Document whose baseUri() equals the provided baseUri and whose body contains a

element with text "hello"
pseudocode: Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(html, baseUri);
assert doc != null; assert doc.baseUri().equals(baseUri); assert doc.body().select("p").text().equals("hello");

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with empty HTML returns a Document with empty body
scenarios.expectedResult: return value is a Document whose body has no child elements
pseudocode: Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(html, baseUri);

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null html throws NullPointerException when creating StringReader
parseInput(Reader, String) with stub TreeBuilder that throws at parse invokes exception path
scenarios.precondition: Parser uses a custom TreeBuilder stub whose parse(Reader,String,Parser) throws IllegalStateException to simulate parse failure
pseudocode: TEST TC04_O2 parseInput Reader parse throws
TreeBuilder stub = new TreeBuilder() { override parse(r,u,p){ throw new IllegalStateException("fail"); } /* other methods stubbed */ };
parser.setTreeBuilder(stub); Reader reader = new StringReader("x"); String baseUri = "http://a/";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with valid Reader returns Document matching input
scenarios.expectedResult: return value is a Document whose body contains a <div> with text "test"
pseudocode: TEST TC05_O2 parseInput Reader valid input

Reader reader = new StringReader("
test
"); String baseUri = "http://example.org/";
Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(reader, baseUri);
